because.text: Third external round, targeting the highest-risk new artifact: the from-scratch Rust Ed25519 verifier (impl-rs/src/ed25519.rs). It found TWO REAL P0s the RFC-TV1 + 452-case differential had not caught -- the bugs live in canonicality/cofactor edges random legitimate inputs never reach. P0-a: pt_decompress accepted the non-canonical identity encoding 0100..0080 because `x == fe_zero()` was a strict array compare that missed an UNREDUCED zero (u=y^2-1 came out as p in limbs); fixed with fe_eq(&x,&fe_zero()). P0-b: verify used the unreduced 512-bit SHA-512 output as the scalar for [k]A, which diverges from RFC 8032 on MIXED-TORSION keys (A=A0+T8 passes the weak-key blocklist since it is not pure small-order; L=5 mod 8 makes [H]A != [H mod L]A); fixed by adding mod_l (bit-by-bit 512-bit reduction mod L) so verify is now the exact RFC cofactorless equation for every key. Benign P2 (fe_mul r1 mask) also fixed. Re-validated: edtest PASS (RFC 8032 TV1 + both P0 regressions + mod_l reference match), all three §8 signatures verify, Ed25519 differential vs Python now 472/472 (200 keypairs x valid/tampered/wrong-msg + small-order + 20 mixed-torsion), three-way canon 43/43, full suite green. Lesson: a large random differential is necessary but not sufficient for from-scratch crypto -- adversarial algebra reaches the edges it cannot. cmd@v1 re-runs conformance PY/GO/RS + edtest + both differentials + the suite; transcript pinned. This completes W3 (a third independent Python+Go+Rust verifier) and the architect backlog.

subject.note: Gemini 3.1 Pro Ed25519 crypto audit: fixed 2 P0s in the from-scratch Rust verifier (non-canonical identity decompress; mixed-torsion k-not-reduced-mod-L). RS now RFC-exact; differential 472/472.
